title: C++ Conditions Overview
content:
### Conditions in C++

Conditional statements allow your program to make decisions and execute different code paths based on boolean expressions.

---

### Types of Conditional Statements

| Statement | Keyword | Use Case |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| If Statement | `if` | Single condition |
| If-Else | `if-else` | Binary decision |
| Else If | `else if` | Multiple conditions |
| Switch | `switch` | Multiple discrete values |
| Ternary | `?:` | Inline condition |

---

### Boolean Expressions

Conditions evaluate to `true` or `false`:

```cpp
int age = 18;
if (age >= 18) {
  cout << "Adult";
}
```

---

### Comparison and Logical Operators

| Category | Operators |
|----------|-----------|
| Comparison | `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=` |
| Logical | `&&` (AND), `||` (OR), `!` (NOT) |

---

> **Key Point:** Any non-zero value is treated as `true` in a condition. Zero, `nullptr`, and empty pointers evaluate to `false`.

quiz.question: Which values evaluate to false in a C++ condition?
